evals.prompt: I want to add a skill rating system to the marketplace — users should be able to rate skills 1-5 stars after using them, and we show the average on the skill listing.
evals.expected_output: A concise spec with user stories, edge cases as bullets, relevant files, no open questions

assertions.text: Spec contains no Gherkin syntax (no Given/When/Then keywords)
Spec contains user stories in 'As a [persona], I want [action] so that [outcome]' format
Spec has no 'Open Questions' section and no unresolved TBD/TODO markers
Edge cases are listed as brief one-line bullets, not full scenarios
Total spec is under 150 lines
Spec lists actual file paths from the codebase
Spec contains acceptance criteria as a checklist

evals.prompt: We need better notifications. Users are missing important stuff.
evals.expected_output: A spec that resolves all ambiguity through discovery questions, produces lean user stories, no open questions left

assertions.text: Spec contains no Gherkin syntax (no Given/When/Then keywords)
Spec contains user stories in 'As a [persona], I want [action] so that [outcome]' format
Spec has no 'Open Questions' section and no unresolved TBD/TODO markers
Edge cases are listed as brief one-line bullets, not full scenarios
Total spec is under 150 lines
Spec lists actual file paths from the codebase
Spec contains acceptance criteria as a checklist
Despite the vague prompt, the spec makes concrete product decisions rather than leaving things generic or unresolved

evals.prompt: Add a bulk import feature — users should be able to upload a CSV of skills to install at once, instead of installing them one by one.
evals.expected_output: A spec with user stories and brief edge case bullets covering validation, partial failures, duplicates

assertions.text: Spec contains no Gherkin syntax (no Given/When/Then keywords)
Spec contains user stories in 'As a [persona], I want [action] so that [outcome]' format
Spec has no 'Open Questions' section and no unresolved TBD/TODO markers
Edge cases are listed as brief one-line bullets, not full scenarios
Total spec is under 150 lines
Spec lists actual file paths from the codebase
Spec contains acceptance criteria as a checklist
